g.text: Suppressed units (ON<OFF), both regions + dHPC up-units at amp100/180.

text.style: font-size: 7.8px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif; fill: #222222

g.text: Pickup can only ADD apparent spikes — it cannot cause suppression.

text.style: font-weight: 700; font-size: 9.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif; text-anchor: start; fill: #7cb342

g.text: LEC population leans down at 50 Hz (10 of 15 units down, mean −0.08 Hz).

text.style: font-size: 7.8px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif; fill: #222222

g.text: A net loss of spikes can't be additive artifact.

g.text: THE EVIDENCE — direction is the discriminator (additive pickup ADDS apparent spikes, doesn't remove them), so suppression is hard to fake;

text.style: font-size: 10.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif

g.text: and the up-going units — incl. unit 87 — PASS the ACG + ISI spike-artifact screens. So the spike-rate effect is not explained by 50 Hz pickup.

g.text: Residual caveat: arousal/state, n=1, indirect sensory-network effects.
